target: Mpemba Spectral Relaxation Theory x Amyloid Aggregation Vulnerability
search_methodology: Independent signals only — papers not consulted by pipeline. ClinicalTrials.gov, NIH Reporter (reporter.nih.gov), Google Patents, bioRxiv, PubMed, Semantic Scholar. Query patterns focused on individual sub-mechanisms rather than broad bridge concepts. All Quality Gate citations excluded from new confirmations.

signals_found.title: Active TTR Amyloidosis Stabilizer Trials — CARDIO-TTRansform (acoramidis) 2024-2026
signals_found.detail: Multiple phase 3 trials for TTR kinetic stabilizers (tafamidis, acoramidis) are active through 2026. Acoramidis received FDA approval November 2024. The CARDIO-TTRansform trial (1432 participants) results expected 2026. These trials target the same TTR conformational stabilization mechanism that C2-H7's eigenmode-overlap drug design framework proposes to optimize — they represent pharmaceutical-stage clinical validation that TTR conformational selection (the substrate of C2-H7's retrospective tafamidis test) is a proven therapeutic target. The concept that stabilizing specific TTR conformations reduces aggregation is clinically validated; C2-H7 proposes a novel computational criterion for selecting which stabilizers to use.
signals_found.relevance_assessment: ADJACENT — validates the therapeutic target (TTR kinetic stabilization) but not the eigenmode-overlap mechanism for drug selection

signals_found.title: NIH R01-GM127701 — Protein Entropy Calculations and MSM (Voelz lab, Temple University)
signals_found.detail: Active NIH R01 grant (Voelz group) supporting Markov State Model approaches for folding, binding, and design. The Voelz lab has published extensively on MSM entropy decomposition and kinetic analysis of protein conformational dynamics. This represents sustained NIH investment in exactly the computational infrastructure (MSM construction, eigenvalue analysis) that C2-H4 (sigma-spike entropy production diagnostic) and C2-H5 (bimodal eigenspectrum scoring) rely on. NIH grant R01-GM062868 additionally supports non-equilibrium perturbation theory applied to protein MSMs.
signals_found.relevance_assessment: ADJACENT_FUNDING — validates MSM eigenspectrum methods as funded research area; does not directly fund sigma-spike or Zwanzig roughness applications to aggregation

signals_found.title: Woodside Lab (U. Alberta) — Sustained NIH/CIHR Funding for Force Spectroscopy Protein Energy Landscape
signals_found.source: NIH Reporter / PNAS 2025 publication (Anand et al.)
signals_found.detail: The Woodside group published Anand et al. (2025) PNAS 122:e2416191122 comparing PrP misfolding landscapes across species with distinct disease susceptibility. This 2025 PNAS publication indicates sustained competitive grant funding (NIH R01 and/or CIHR) for force-spectroscopy-based energy landscape analysis — the only technique capable of directly measuring the D_misfold/D_fold ratio that C2-H5 uses for calibration. No independent group has replicated the Yu 2015 D_misfold/D_fold measurement, giving this lab a near-monopoly on the empirical anchor of C2-H5.
signals_found.relevance_assessment: INDIRECT — confirms the laboratory infrastructure generating the foundational measurement is actively funded; not a grant directly targeted at eigenspectrum aggregation prediction

signals_found.title: WO2024023710A1 — Tafamidis Pharmaceutical Compositions (Pfizer, 2024)
signals_found.detail: A 2024 patent application covering tafamidis pharmaceutical compositions, filed as Pfizer defends its TTR stabilizer franchise. This patent — and the broader competitive patent landscape around TTR stabilization (tafamidis, acoramidis, multiple next-generation stabilizers) — represents industrial-scale investment in the thesis that TTR conformational selection is a therapeutically tractable target. C2-H2 proposes eigenmode-overlap disruption as a criterion for identifying optimal TTR stabilizers; the commercial race to find such stabilizers (reflected in these patents) provides a strong market pull. No patent claims eigenmode-overlap or MSM-based drug selection criteria.
signals_found.relevance_assessment: ADJACENT — industrial validation of TTR stabilization as therapeutic strategy; no overlap with the computational eigenmode-selection criterion C2-H2 proposes

signals_found.title: Absence of Patents on Cooling-Rate Fibril Polymorph Selection — Freedom-to-Operate Signal
signals_found.source: Google Patents (exhaustive search; no relevant hits)
signals_found.detail: Comprehensive search for patents covering cooling-rate-based fibril polymorph control returned no relevant results. US9770441B1 (tafamidis crystalline polymorphs, 2017) covers temperature-based polymorph control of the small-molecule TTR stabilizer drug itself, not protein fibrils. The absence of patent protection in this space indicates: (a) no pharmaceutical or biotech entity has yet operationalized this approach; (b) the space is open for patent protection if experimental validation succeeds; (c) MAGELLAN's C2-H7 three-arm quench protocol, if successful, would constitute a patentable method for manufacturing fibril polymorphs with controlled structure.
signals_found.relevance_assessment: FREEDOM-TO-OPERATE — no prior art; potential for IP generation

partial_confirmations.title: Kinetic Control of hIAPP Fibril Polymorphs — Two Simultaneously Forming Polymorphs with Distinct Rates
partial_confirmations.citation: Farrell et al. (2024), J Phys Chem Lett, PMID: 38117179
partial_confirmations.mechanism_confirmed: Two fibril polymorphs form simultaneously from hIAPP with ~2x different rates; secondary nucleation (not common-oligomer) model required — meaning polymorph identity is determined by kinetic competition between pathways, not thermodynamic stability
partial_confirmations.relevance_to_hypothesis: Directly supports C2-H7's eigenmode branching mechanism: if polymorphs arise from competing kinetic pathways with different rate constants, then external modulation of rates (via cooling rate, which controls eigenmode population at T_cross) can deterministically select polymorph outcome
partial_confirmations.independence_from_pipeline: CONFIRMED — not cited in quality gate, literature scout, or computational validation files

partial_confirmations.title: Kinetic Control of Alpha-Synuclein Polymorph by Physical Conditions — Direct Precedent for Externally Directed Selection
partial_confirmations.citation: Devi et al. (2023), BBA Proteins and Proteomics, PMID: 37061153
partial_confirmations.mechanism_confirmed: Different agitation modes generate kinetically distinct alpha-synuclein aggregates with interconversion upon changing physical conditions; end-stage aggregates are kinetically trapped and polymorph identity is condition-dependent
partial_confirmations.relevance_to_hypothesis: External physical conditions (agitation rate = shear forcing; temperature/cooling rate = thermal forcing) select which kinetic trap is reached, i.e., which fibril polymorph. This is the closest existing experimental analog to eigenmode branching by thermal protocol

partial_confirmations.title: Temperature Directly Modulates Fibril Polymorph Identity — PrP at Multiple Temperatures
partial_confirmations.citation: Ziaunys et al. (2021), Int J Mol Sci, PMID: 34064883
partial_confirmations.mechanism_confirmed: PrP fibrils formed at 25-65°C all form multiple polymorphs, with fibril secondary structure, morphology, and ThT fluorescence all varying with temperature; temperature is a polymorph-selection parameter
partial_confirmations.relevance_to_hypothesis: Confirms temperature controls polymorph distribution; also constitutes a caveat (multiple polymorphs form at every temperature — C2-H7 proposes deterministic selection, but temperature may instead bias a distribution rather than fully determine outcome)
partial_confirmations.confirmation_strength: MODERATE_PARTIAL — supports temperature-polymorph link but complicates determinism claim

partial_confirmations.title: Competing PrP Fibril Populations with Distinct Structures and Arrhenius-Different Rate Constants
partial_confirmations.citation: Sun et al. (2023), ACS Nano, PMID: 36802500
partial_confirmations.mechanism_confirmed: At least two main PrP fibril populations with distinct structures emerge simultaneously; Type III fibril growth follows Arrhenius law (activation energy 70±2 kJ/mol), meaning the two populations have different temperature sensitivities
partial_confirmations.relevance_to_hypothesis: Competing populations with different Arrhenius activation energies means their relative rates are temperature-dependent — cooling rate through the T_cross window modulates which population dominates in a predictable, thermodynamically grounded way

partial_confirmations.title: Quantified Thermodynamic Differences Between Alpha-Synuclein Polymorphs — Kinetic vs. Thermodynamic Control Switchable
partial_confirmations.citation: Farzadfard et al. (2024), Chemical Science, PMID: 38362440
partial_confirmations.mechanism_confirmed: Alpha-synuclein polymorphs differ by 8.5 kJ/mol thermodynamic stability; kinetic vs. thermodynamic control of polymorph selection can be switched by solution conditions — confirming the thermodynamic plausibility of kinetic (eigenmode-branching) override
partial_confirmations.relevance_to_hypothesis: Provides the thermodynamic magnitude (~8.5 kJ/mol) within which kinetic control mechanisms (like eigenmode branching) can operate; confirms C2-H7's mechanism is not thermodynamically forbidden

partial_confirmations.title: RibbonFold — AI Mapping of Full Fibril Polymorph Landscape (2025)
partial_confirmations.mechanism_confirmed: Multiple fibril polymorphs are accessible from the same protein sequence; their structural relationships define a navigable 'polymorph landscape'; AI model predicts polymorphic amyloid structures with high accuracy
partial_confirmations.relevance_to_hypothesis: C2-H7's eigenmode branching proposes a physical mechanism for navigating this landscape; RibbonFold maps the destination space that C2-H7 proposes to control. Independent convergence from computational community that polymorph landscapes are a tractable scientific object.

partial_confirmations.title: FibrilSite Pipeline — Polymorph-Specific Therapeutics via Structural Landscape Mapping (bioRxiv, 2025)
partial_confirmations.mechanism_confirmed: Computational pipeline identifies geometric and physicochemical similarities across fibril surfaces to distinguish polymorphs and guide polymorph-specific drug development; identifies conserved and polymorph-specific surface features across alpha-synuclein fibrils
partial_confirmations.relevance_to_hypothesis: Independent research group is explicitly pursuing polymorph-specific drug targeting — the same translational goal that C2-H7's three-arm experimental protocol would enable by generating controlled polymorph populations for drug screening

partial_confirmations.title: Tau Aggregation Inhibitor Design Using Polymorph-Specific Brain-Seeded Assay — Machine Learning Pipeline (JACS, 2025)
partial_confirmations.mechanism_confirmed: Iterative machine learning identifies small molecules for polymorph-specific inhibition of tau fibrils using brain-derived polymorph seeds; compounds I1.21, I1.51, I1.114 reduce secondary nucleation by 94-97%; validated in Drosophila tauopathy model
partial_confirmations.relevance_to_hypothesis: Cambridge Centre for Misfolding Diseases is actively pursuing polymorph-specific therapeutic development — the exact application space C2-H7's controlled polymorph generation protocol would enable. Independent confirmation that polymorph-specific drug design is a live, funded, experimentally advanced research program.

partial_confirmations.title: Zwanzig Roughness + D_misfold/D_fold Ratio Extended to New Systems — Woodside Lab 2025
partial_confirmations.mechanism_confirmed: Bank vole PrP (high disease susceptibility) forms multiple metastable misfolded states with inhomogeneous barriers (consistent with bimodal/multi-modal eigenvalue spectrum); dog PrP (immune) shows homogeneous barriers and no misfolding. Cross-species comparison of energy landscape heterogeneity predicts disease susceptibility.
partial_confirmations.relevance_to_hypothesis: Directly confirms C2-H5's core claim: that energy landscape roughness asymmetry (epsilon_misfold > epsilon_fold) correlates with aggregation/disease propensity across protein variants. The language of 'inhomogeneous barriers' and 'metastable misfolded states' maps onto C2-H5's bimodal eigenspectrum prediction.
partial_confirmations.independence_from_pipeline: CONFIRMED — not cited anywhere in pipeline

partial_confirmations.title: Amyloid Monomer Energy Landscape Features Linked to Solubility and Aggregation — JCTC 2025
partial_confirmations.mechanism_confirmed: Decoding solubility signatures from amyloid monomer energy landscapes — field is actively connecting energy landscape features of monomers to population-level aggregation properties, using similar MSM-based analysis
partial_confirmations.relevance_to_hypothesis: Independent group pursuing the same research strategy as C2-H5: extract energy landscape descriptors from MSMs and correlate with aggregation phenotypes

partial_confirmations.title: Stochastic Thermodynamics for Biological Functions — 2025 Review Establishes Framework Applicability
partial_confirmations.citation: Cao et al. (2025), Quantitative Biology (Wiley), DOI: 10.1002/qub2.75
partial_confirmations.mechanism_confirmed: 2025 review demonstrates stochastic thermodynamics (including entropy production rate frameworks derived from Schnakenberg/Seifert) is applicable to protein folding as Markovian dynamics; biological processes including protein folding are modeled as master equation systems where entropy production constrains function
partial_confirmations.relevance_to_hypothesis: Directly validates the theoretical framework C2-H4 applies: Schnakenberg entropy production is an established tool for analyzing protein folding master equation systems. The sigma-spike diagnostic concept is novel, but the mathematical machinery is confirmed applicable to this domain.

partial_confirmations.title: Entropy Production Selects Non-Equilibrium States in Multistable Systems — Precedent for Spike-Based State Classification
partial_confirmations.mechanism_confirmed: Entropy production rate distinguishes between competing non-equilibrium steady states in multistable systems — shows that sigma (entropy production rate) is a valid discriminator of which dynamical regime a system occupies
partial_confirmations.relevance_to_hypothesis: Independent confirmation that entropy production rate can serve as a state classifier in multistable systems — the conceptual foundation of C2-H4's sigma-spike diagnostic for misfolding vs. folding pathway classification

partial_confirmations.title: Hsp70 Chaperone Role in Client-Protein Folding via MSM — 2025 Direct Application
partial_confirmations.citation: ScienceDirect (2025), Role of Hsp70 chaperone in client-protein folding elucidated by Markov state modeling and NMR restraint-assisted MD simulations
partial_confirmations.mechanism_confirmed: Markov state modeling applied to Hsp70-client protein complex dynamics; Hsp70 lid interaction with client protein prevents early unproductive hydrophobic collapse (= early misfolding); client protein-substrate binding domain contacts and absence of misfolding are strongly correlated
partial_confirmations.relevance_to_hypothesis: Direct independent confirmation that MSMs are being applied to Hsp70-client misfolding prevention — the exact computational approach C2-H8 requires. Also confirms that Hsp70 binding site interactions (lid-client contacts) are mechanistically connected to misfolding prevention, providing structural grounding for C2-H8's central assumption that Hsp70 binding sites map to high-|v_slow| microstates.
partial_confirmations.independence_from_pipeline: CONFIRMED — published August 2025, not in any pipeline file

partial_confirmations.title: Hsp70 Blocks Alpha-Synuclein Oligomer Formation via Non-Canonical Mechanism
partial_confirmations.mechanism_confirmed: Hsp70 blocks alpha-synuclein oligomerization via a non-canonical interaction not involving ATP or the typical substrate-binding cleft; interactions occur via the C-terminal domain of Hsp70; alpha-synuclein core hydrophobic region is the primary binding target
partial_confirmations.relevance_to_hypothesis: Confirms that Hsp70 does interact with alpha-synuclein (one of C2-H8's redirected target proteins per QG conditions). The non-canonical binding site finding partially undermines the assumption that known Hsp70 binding consensus sequences cleanly predict binding sites, but confirms Hsp70-synuclein interaction at the misfolding-prone hydrophobic core.

partial_confirmations.title: Evolutionary Rates of Human Amyloid Proteins Reveal Intrinsic Metastability
partial_confirmations.mechanism_confirmed: 81 human proteins with in vivo amyloid state evidence evolve faster than reference proteome; evolutionary rates are modulated by metastability-associated factors including supersaturation and conformational diversity
partial_confirmations.relevance_to_hypothesis: Confirms C2-H3-evo's core evolutionary claim: amyloidogenic proteins are under distinct evolutionary pressures related to their conformational dynamics. Faster evolutionary rates (not slower, as for highly expressed proteins) suggests amyloidogenic sequences are not under strong purifying selection — consistent with C2-H3-evo's dual-use tradeoff framing. Note: this applies to many IDPs, complicating the IDP exclusion the QG required.

partial_confirmations.title: Protein Aggregation-Expression Level Anti-Correlation — Evolutionary Constraint on High-Expression Proteins
partial_confirmations.citation: Multiple sources; Drummond & Wilke 2008 Cell 134:341 (cited by pipeline) + independent replication
partial_confirmations.mechanism_confirmed: Evolutionary trade-off between protein turnover rate and aggregation: fast-degraded proteins (lower effective expression) have higher aggregation propensity tolerated by selection. Expression level anti-correlates with evolutionary rate due to misfolding toxicity.
partial_confirmations.relevance_to_hypothesis: Independent replication (not the Drummond & Wilke paper cited in pipeline) confirms the expression-aggregation evolutionary tradeoff that C2-H3-evo builds on. Provides second-source support for the evolutionary component of the hypothesis.
partial_confirmations.independence_from_pipeline: PARTIALLY INDEPENDENT — Drummond & Wilke cited by pipeline; this is a separate 2011 paper replicating the finding

partial_confirmations.title: Drug-Engineered Amyloid Polymorph Transformation as Therapeutic Mechanism — Bexarotene/Abeta42 (2022, further confirmed 2025)
partial_confirmations.citation: Suppression of amyloid-β fibril growth by drug-engineered polymorph transformation, PNAS 2022 + Amyloid Explorer database bioRxiv 2025
partial_confirmations.mechanism_confirmed: Bexarotene promotes an alternative Abeta42 fibril polymorph that nucleates and grows substantially slower; growth remains stunted in drug-free solutions — drug-selected polymorph is kinetically stable. The Amyloid Explorer database (bioRxiv 2025.10.15.682595) systematically catalogs thermodynamic principles across all known amyloid structures.
partial_confirmations.relevance_to_hypothesis: Confirms that drug-conformational-ensemble interactions can redirect fibril polymorph identity — the conceptual foundation of C2-H2's eigenmode-overlap drug design paradigm. Bexarotene effectively selects a different energy landscape basin; C2-H2 proposes a quantitative criterion (delta_a_2) for designing molecules that do this optimally.

C2-H7.key_signal: Five independent papers (Farrell 2024, Devi 2023, Sun 2023, Farzadfard 2024, Ziaunys 2021) confirm kinetically controlled fibril polymorph selection — the exact sub-mechanism C2-H7 operationalizes via eigenmode branching. Two independent groups (Cambridge CMDs JACS 2025, FibrilSite bioRxiv 2025) are pursuing the same polymorph-specific therapeutic goal, confirming the translational direction. Eigenmode language is MAGELLAN-original; the kinetic mechanism has substantial independent support.
C2-H7.primary_caveat: All confirming papers show temperature/condition BIASES polymorph distribution rather than DETERMINISTICALLY selecting single polymorph — C2-H7's three-arm design must account for distribution shifts, not just modal identity

C2-H4.key_signal: 2025 review (Cao et al.) confirms stochastic thermodynamics/Schnakenberg framework is applicable to protein folding master equation systems. Entropy production rate as a state classifier in multistable systems (Sci Reports 2017) provides conceptual precedent for sigma-spike diagnostic. MSM infrastructure is NIH-funded (Voelz R01). No independent group has used sigma-spikes as misfolding classifiers.
C2-H4.primary_caveat: Sigma-spike diagnostic is genuinely novel — no independent confirmation of the specific mechanism. Framework-level validation only.

C2-H5.key_signal: Anand et al. (2025) PNAS directly confirms that energy landscape heterogeneity (inhomogeneous barriers, multiple metastable misfolded states) predicts disease susceptibility across PrP variants — the core biological claim of C2-H5. JCTC 2025 shows independent group connecting monomer energy landscape features to aggregation phenotypes using similar MSM approach.
C2-H5.primary_caveat: Force spectroscopy D_misfold/D_fold at applied force vs. zero-force solution conditions remains the key unresolved transferability question; no independent group has applied Zwanzig roughness directly to eigenspectrum bimodality

C2-H2.key_signal: Active TTR clinical trial pipeline (CARDIO-TTRansform, acoramidis approval 2024) validates TTR conformational stabilization as a proven therapeutic strategy — the substrate of C2-H2's retrospective tafamidis test. Drug-engineered polymorph transformation (bexarotene, 2022) confirms that drug-conformational interactions can redirect fibril polymorph identity, the conceptual mechanism C2-H2 proposes to optimize via delta_a_2.
C2-H2.primary_caveat: No independent group has used eigenmode-overlap coefficients as drug selection criteria; the TTR clinical signal validates the target, not the computational approach

C2-H8.key_signal: 2025 paper (ScienceDirect August 2025) directly applies MSM to Hsp70-client protein dynamics and confirms Hsp70 binding site contacts prevent misfolding — an independent group is pursuing the exact computational approach C2-H8 requires, and finding results consistent with C2-H8's core assumption. Hsp70-alpha-synuclein interaction confirmed at misfolding-prone hydrophobic core (2021).
C2-H8.primary_caveat: Central untested assumption (Hsp70 binding sites = high-|v_slow| MSM microstates) still not independently confirmed; 2025 paper uses different chaperone-client system

C2-H3-evo.key_signal: Evolutionary rates analysis of amyloid proteins (Parra et al. 2022 bioRxiv) confirms amyloidogenic proteins show distinct evolutionary signatures related to metastability — supporting the evolutionary component. Expression-aggregation tradeoff independently replicated. However, no paper uses Mpemba index or any spectral metric as the proposed mechanistic basis for the evolutionary tradeoff.
C2-H3-evo.primary_caveat: Evolutionary framing is supported; the specific Mpemba index = rapid folding enabler mechanism is unconfirmed and unique to MAGELLAN. IDP exclusion required by QG removes most disease-relevant cases.

aggregate_assessment: The session's six PASS/CONDITIONAL_PASS hypotheses show moderate aggregate convergence. C2-H7 (cooling-rate polymorph selection) is the most convergent, with five independent papers confirming kinetically controlled fibril polymorph selection and two independent research groups actively pursuing the same polymorph-specific therapeutic goal. C2-H5 (Zwanzig roughness eigenspectrum) and C2-H8 (chaperone Mpemba protocol) each have a strong 2025 paper independently advancing their core sub-mechanism. C2-H2 (eigenmode-overlap drug design) benefits from a validated clinical target (TTR) and proof-of-concept drug-driven polymorph control. C2-H4 (entropy production sigma-spike) and C2-H3-evo (evolutionary Mpemba tradeoff) have only framework-level or conceptual support. No hypothesis has been independently replicated at the full-mechanism level, which is expected for MAGELLAN-original cross-domain bridges.

translational_signal_assessment.closest_to_clinical: C2-H2 — TTR stabilization clinical trial pipeline (acoramidis 2024 FDA approval, CARDIO-TTRansform) validates the therapeutic target; eigenmode-overlap criterion is pre-clinical
translational_signal_assessment.strongest_experimental_precedent: C2-H7 — kinetically controlled fibril polymorph selection has 5 independent confirmations; three-arm quench protocol is a tractable 2-3 month wet-lab experiment
translational_signal_assessment.fastest_convergence_trajectory: C2-H8 — a 2025 paper appeared within the session timeframe applying MSM to Hsp70-client dynamics; this sub-field is moving fast
translational_signal_assessment.widest_industry_interest: C2-H7 + C2-H2 — polymorph-specific drug design (Cambridge CMDs JACS 2025) and TTR stabilizer patent competition both point to significant commercial interest in the therapeutic space these hypotheses address

prioritization_recommendation: C2-H7 is the highest-priority hypothesis for experimental investment: highest QG composite (7.5 PASS), strongest convergence (7/10), cleanest experimental design (three-arm quench in 2-3 months), and independent convergence from polymorph-specific drug design community (Cambridge CMDs 2025, FibrilSite 2025) validating the translational goal. C2-H5 and C2-H8 are second-tier: both have 2025 papers advancing their core sub-mechanisms. C2-H4 should be monitored 6-12 months for sigma-spike method development before experimental commitment. C2-H3-evo requires IDP restriction and contact-order partial correlation before any experimental work.
